story: New snow sharpened the trail. Makula, hunting the slayer of his life‑mate Lillus, followed through the forest. At dusk a winter‑hare sprang; iron jaws snapped from the snow and crushed it. Makula read the danger and moved on. Night silvered the drifts. Smoke and fire-scent came on the wind. In a knot of stunted firs a campfire glowed, the man sitting alone by it. Between the last firs and the fire, the open snow hid a brush‑latticed pit—fresh‑cut branches roofed a man‑dug hole, the surface smoothed clean and white. Ears flat, body low, he slid between the trees, vengeance heating his blood.
implausible_knowledge: Between the last firs and the fire, the open snow hid a brush‑latticed pit—fresh‑cut branches roofed a man‑dug hole, the surface smoothed clean and white.
question: At the edge of the firs with the lone fire ahead and his prey before it, what will Makula do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Try other plans to skirt the hole.
answer_without_knowledge: Launch straight from the trees in a direct leap at the man.
